title: QIAGEN DNeasy PowerSoil Kit
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"438h9","text":"For the isolation of microbial genomic DNA from all soil types","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eau3p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3f34s","text":"The DNeasy PowerSoil Kit comprises a novel and proprietary method for isolating genomic DNA from environmental samples using patented Inhibitor Removal Technology® (IRT). This kit is intended for use with environmental samples containing high humic acid content, including difficult soil types such as compost, sediment and manure. Other more common soil types have also been used successfully with this kit. The isolated DNA has a high level of purity, which allows for more successful PCR amplification of organisms from the sample. PCR analysis has been performed to detect a variety of organisms including bacteria (e.g., Bacillus subtilis, Bacillus anthracis), fungi (e.g., yeasts, molds), algae and actinomycetes (e.g., Streptomyces).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dtggm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4nlf4","text":"Protocol used successfully to detect target fish species sedDNA by Olajos et al., 2018; Nelson-Chorney et al., 2019; Thomson-Laing et al., 2020; Shiragaki et al., 2021; and Naro-Maciel et al., 2022 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"u2lt","text":"Perform all centrifugation steps at room temperature (15–25°C).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"519v1","text":"If Solution C1 has precipitated, heat at 60°C until precipitate dissolves.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"erdcb","text":"Solution C5 contains ethanol. It is flammable.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bkfep","text":"Do not use bleach to clean the inside of the QIAvac® 24 Plus manifold.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: ADD 0.25 g of soil sample to the PowerBead Tube VORTEX gently to mix
ADD 60 µL of Solution C1 INVERT several times or vortex briefly
SECURE PowerBead Tubes horizontally using a Vortex Adapter for 24 (1.5–2.0 ml) tubes VORTEX at maximum speed for 00:10:00
CENTRIFUGE tubes at 10.000 x g, , for 00:00:30 TRANSFER supernatant to a clean 2 mL Collection Tube
ADD 250 µL of Solution C2 VORTEX for 00:00:05 INCUBATE at 2 °C to 8 °C for 00:05:00
CENTRIFUGE tubes at 10000 x g, , for 00:01:00 AVOIDING the pellet, transfer up to 600 µL of supernatant to a clean 2 mL Collection Tube
SHAKE to mix Solution C4 ADD 1200 µL of Solution C4 to the supernatant VORTEX for 00:00:05
LOAD 675 µL onto an MB Spin Column CENTRIFUGE at 10.000 x g, , for 00:01:00 DISCARD liquid flow-through
REPEAT step 10 twice, until all of the sample has been processed
ADD 500 µL of Solution C5 CENTRIFUGE at 10.000 x g, , for 00:00:30 DISCARD liquid flow-through
CENTRIFUGE again at 10.000 x g, , 00:01:00 CAREFULLY place the MB Spin Column into a clean 2 mL Collection Tube. Avoid splashing any residual Solution C5 onto the column
ADD 100 µL of Solution C6 to the center of the white filter membrane CENTRIFUGE at 10000 x g for 00:00:30 DISCARD the MB Spin Column DNA is now ready for downstream applications
ADD 200 µL of Solution C3 VORTEX briefly to mix INCUBATE at 2 °C to 8 °C for 00:05:00
CENTRIFUGE tubes at 10000 x g for 00:01:00 AVOIDING the pellet, transfer up to 750 µL of supernatant to a clean 2 mL Collection Tube
creator: Yuanyu Cheng
